samples.name: en_30s
samples.reference: The Evening of the Betrothal. Villefort had, as we have said, hastened back to Madame de Saint-Méran's in the Place du Grand Cours, and on entering the house found that the guests whom he had left at table were taking coffee in the salon. Renée was, with all the rest of the company, anxiously awaiting him, and his entrance was followed by a general exclamation. "Well, Decapitator, Guardian of the State, Royalist, Brutus, what is the matter?" said
samples.source: LibriVox - The Count of Monte Cristo, Chapter 9 (truncated)

results.hypothesis: The evening of the betrothal. Beaufort had, as we have said, hastened back to Madame de Saint Marle's, in the Place de Grand Couer, and on entering the house, found that the guests whom he had left at table were taking coffee in the salon. Rene was with all the rest of the company anxiously awaiting him, and his entrance was followed by a general exclamation. "Well, decapitator, guardian of the state, royalist, Brutus, what is the matter?" said.

results.hypothesis: The evening of the betrothal. Fieldor had, as we have said, hastened back to Madame de Saint Maraz in the Place de Grancourt, and on entering the house found that the guests whom he had left at table were taking coffee in this. René was, with all the rest of the company, anxiously awaiting him. On his entrance, was followed by a general exclamation: "Well, decapitator, guardian of the state, royalist Brutus." What's the matter? Said.
